title: Sky‑high losses: Iran war drives airlines to biggest crash since Covid – $50bn gone
content: Global airlines face their worst financial crisis since COVID-19 due to the Iran conflict, wiping over $50 billion off major carriers' market value. Soaring jet fuel prices, now double, threaten ticket price hikes and potential shortages. Executives warn of dampened demand from sustained high fares, while Middle Eastern airlines are hit particularly hard by airspace closures and tourism collapse.

title: IPL 2026: Five pacers in race as KKR look for Harshit Rana replacement
content: Kolkata Knight Riders are scrambling for replacements ahead of IPL 2026 after Harshit Rana's injury. Mid-preseason trials at Eden Gardens are underway, with Navdeep Saini and Akash Madhwal among those being assessed. The team also faces concerns with Mustafizur Rahman's departure and Matheesha Pathirana's delayed return, intensifying their bowling worries.

title: Under the sea: How Iran's invisible fleet of 'midget submarines' threatens Strait of Hormuz
content: As Middle East tensions rise, Iran's hidden underwater threats, particularly its Ghadir-class midget submarines and extensive naval mine stockpiles, pose a significant challenge to global shipping. These stealthy assets, capable of operating in shallow waters and laying mines, can disrupt vital oil transit routes like the Strait of Hormuz, impacting global trade far more effectively than visible attacks.

title: The 3-word message Steve Jobs sent to his employees the day Apple beat Microsoft
content: In 2010, Apple's market capitalization surpassed Microsoft's, a significant achievement after its near-bankruptcy in 1997. Steve Jobs, acknowledging the milestone, quoted Walt Disney to emphasize the company's focus on future innovation. While Apple and Alphabet now trail Nvidia, this 2010 event marked a pivotal shift in tech leadership.

title: MBBS graduate ends life after family objects to adopting cat in Hyderabad
content: A 23-year-old MBBS graduate in Hyderabad died by suicide after a dispute with her family over keeping a pet cat. The family reportedly opposed the cat due to allergy concerns. Upset by the disagreement, she took her own life while her family was out.

title: Kerala: Woman dresses kids in suits, makes them wear goggles before killing them; 5 found dead
content: A grim discovery in Kochi's Vaduthala saw five family members, including three children, found dead in their rented home. Preliminary findings suggest poisoning for the children, while the women were found hanging. A suicide note points to immense personal distress, including the recent death of the husband. Investigations are ongoing.

title: Elon Musk's SpaceX sends email to FCC, rejects Jeff Bezos' Blue Origin application
content: SpaceX has formally responded to Amazon's FCC petition against its satellite data center proposal. SpaceX argues that if Amazon's criticisms are applied, they must also be applied to Blue Origin's similar satellite data center application. SpaceX is pushing for equitable treatment and consistent review of both companies' plans.

title: ‘Dhurandhar 2’ box office collection day 3: Film crosses Rs 500 crore mark
content: Ranveer Singh's spy thriller 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge' has shattered box office records, crossing the ₹500 crore mark globally in just three days. The Aditya Dhar-directed film, following the journey of an undercover spy infiltrating Lyari's underworld, has garnered immense audience appreciation for its high-octane action and compelling narrative.

title: Iran reacts to Trump's Hormuz warning, threatens US facility in Middle East if energy sites attacked
content: Iran has warned it will retaliate beyond military targets if its energy infrastructure is attacked, signaling a potential escalation. This comes as the US reviews military options, including possible ground troop deployment, though President Trump downplays such a move.  The Pentagon is preparing forces and planning for contingencies, underscoring heightened regional tensions.

title: '$500 billion in one campus': What SoftBank CEO said about OpenAI's biggest AI data centre
content: SoftBank is investing a colossal $500 billion into a single AI data center campus in Ohio, potentially the largest construction project in US history. Part of the 'Stargate' venture, this massive facility aims to power millions of homes and is seen as crucial in the AI race with China. Initial costs are estimated between $30-40 billion.

title: Rohit, Pandya out! Ex-England star names 'worst decision' in history
content: Kevin Pietersen unveiled his all-time IPL XI, notably excluding stars like Rohit Sharma. He lauded Yuzvendra Chahal, calling his release by RCB the league's worst decision. Pietersen's XI featured Gayle, Kohli, de Villiers, Raina, Dhoni, Russell, Jadeja, Narine, Chahal, Malinga, and Bumrah, prioritising impact over strict overseas limits.

title: Dubai Airports update: Air India announces 50 flights; IndiGo issues fresh advisory
content: Dubai airports are experiencing delays and schedule changes due to escalating Middle East tensions. Indian carriers IndiGo and Air India are operating select flights on March 22, 2026, with advisories urging passengers to check flight status and arrive early. Both airlines emphasize passenger and crew safety amidst the uncertain regional situation.

title: A Rajya Sabha rummy: How BJP manages ‘floating majority’ with bellwether backings
content: ​When the BJP swept to power in 2014 with a decisive majority in the Lok Sabha, it did not carry that dominance into the Rajya Sabha. The Upper House, by design, is insulated from electoral waves. Its members are elected by state assemblies, and their terms are staggered, ensuring continuity and preventing sudden shifts. This meant that even as the BJP commanded brute strength in the Lower House, it remained a minority in the Upper House for years. That imbalance mattered!

title: Row erupts in Mumbai civic body after AIMIM chairperson displays husband’s photo
content: AIMIM's M East ward committee chairperson Khairunnisa Akbar Hussain faced controversy after displaying her husband's photograph in her office. The BJP objected, leading to the photo's removal. Hussain stated party workers placed the picture and she had it taken down upon notification, feeling the issue was exaggerated.

title: 'Looks so nice!': First Vande Bharat sleeper's new design leaves internet super impressed
content: India's Vande Bharat Sleeper train is a social media sensation. Travelers are sharing positive reviews of its food, interiors, and cleanliness. The train offers an upgraded passenger experience with features like ergonomic berths and modern lighting. The first service connects Howrah and Kamakhya. Indian Railways plans more sleeper trains by the end of 2026.

title: Ahmedabad doctor attacked while treating patient; gets 16 stitches
content: A 64-year-old doctor was brutally attacked with a steel walking stick and required 16 stitches after intervening in an argument between an injured patient and another man. The assailant also threatened the doctor and other bystanders who attempted to help, leading to further injuries.

title: CSK's playing XI for IPL 2026: MS Dhoni as impact sub, no place for Sarfaraz?
content: Chennai Super Kings gear up for IPL 2026 with a new look, led by Ruturaj Gaikwad. A blockbuster trade brings Sanju Samson aboard, while young talents like Ayush Mhatre and Kartik Sharma are set to shine. Despite Nathan Ellis's injury, CSK boasts a balanced squad, aiming for a record sixth title.

title: Iran invasion next? Pentagon plans for deployment of US troops on ground - report
content: The Pentagon has reportedly developed detailed plans for a potential deployment of US ground troops into Iran amidst ongoing conflict. Senior military commanders have made specific readiness requests, while President Trump has denied immediate plans but kept the option open. Preparations include discussions on detaining Iranian personnel and readying key military unit.

title: Waking up tired even after sleep? Doctor shares tips on how to cure it
content: A unique sensation of 'slow morning' fatigue has surfaced, setting itself apart from ordinary weariness. Healthcare professionals warn this might indicate metabolic fatigue, where the body finds it hard to generate energy. Although high-protein diets have gained popularity as a remedy, experts assert that they do not address deeper issues like hormonal imbalances or insulin resistance.

title: Watch: Iranian missile strikes Jerusalem near Al-Aqsa mosque during Eid prayers
content: Jerusalem's Old City experienced a fragment of an Iranian ballistic missile landing near its holiest sites during Eid al-Fitr. While Israel's air defense intercepted the main threat, debris impacted a parking lot close to the Western Wall and Al-Aqsa Mosque. This incident, occurring amidst escalating regional tensions, highlights Iran's alleged indiscriminate missile fire, as confirmed by the IDF.

title: Oil prices cross $100 — what lies ahead as the Middle East crisis intensifies?
content: Middle East crisis fuels oil market volatility, with prices holding above $100 due to disrupted supplies and infrastructure damage. Experts warn of prolonged high prices, potentially lasting years, as key shipping routes remain impacted. Airlines are preparing for sustained elevated fuel costs, while LNG exports face significant cuts.

title: 'Iss baar No. 6': Rohit Sharma's big promise to Mumbai Indians fans - watch
content: Mumbai Indians captain Rohit Sharma has vowed to bring home their sixth IPL title, addressing fans at a recent event.  Meanwhile, former cricketer Irfan Pathan argues that MI edges out CSK as the greatest IPL team due to their significant contribution in producing top Indian cricketers.

title: Eid in Delhi's Uttam Nagar: Residents shower rose petals on security personnel
content: Eid morning in Uttam Nagar saw an unusual stillness with heavy police presence and controlled access following recent tensions. Worshippers offered prayers peacefully under tight security, with drones overhead. A spontaneous gesture of rose petals showered on police broke the tension, highlighting community appreciation for ensured safety.

title: Several Assam Police commandos injured in suspected ULFA(I) attack in Tinsukia
content: Assam Police commandos sustained injuries in an early Sunday morning attack by suspected ULFA(I) militants in Tinsukia district. The militants reportedly initiated the assault with grenades before opening indiscriminate fire on the security personnel. A search operation has been launched to apprehend the perpetrators.

title: 'Will obliterate power plants': Trump gives Iran 48-hour ultimatum to open Strait of Hormuz
content: US President Trump has issued a stark ultimatum to Iran, demanding the immediate reopening of the Strait of Hormuz within 48 hours or face the obliteration of its power plants. Tehran's military has vowed retaliation, warning of attacks on US infrastructure if its energy facilities are targeted. This escalation comes amid rising oil prices

title: Storm, lightning strikes claim 6 lives across Bihar
content: Six people, including two women, died in separate rain, storm, and lightning incidents across Bihar in the past 24 hours. Two farmers and a student were killed by lightning strikes in Gaya and Jamui districts. Storms and rain also caused house collapses in Araria, resulting in the deaths of two young children.

title: Internet swoons over ‘Army beauty’ — but Jessica Foster isn’t real
content: A viral online persona, Jessica Foster, presented as a patriotic Army woman, has been exposed as an AI-generated fake. Experts highlight this as a growing strategy by right-wing accounts to blend patriotism with explicit content, using convincing AI imagery to attract viewers, monetize interest, and promote political agendas.

title: 'Struck her with axe': Man kills pregnant wife after she refuses to cook spinach
content: A man in Khargone district has been arrested for allegedly killing his pregnant wife after she refused to cook spinach. The accused, Sunil, reportedly attacked his 25-year-old wife, Sheela Bai, with an axe in a fit of rage. The couple had recently returned from working as labourers in Maharashtra.

title: Ahmedabad’s ‘Monkey Man’ on how kindness altered his destiny
content: For 17 years, Swapnil Soni has dedicated his mornings to feeding stray dogs and hundreds of monkeys in Ahmedabad. Inspired by his family's kindness and a 107-year-old man, Soni's regular acts of compassion have led to a belief that his good deeds have brought him success and protection, even after surviving three heart attacks.

title: Diesel price: Bulk diesel price hike rolled back for Gujarat fishermen, will be treated as special exception
content: Gujarat fishermen will continue to receive subsidised diesel after the Centre rolled back a steep Rs 22.43 per litre price hike for bulk fuel. The state government's intervention successfully shielded the fishing community from increased costs, ensuring diesel remains at previously agreed concessional rates.

title: ‘Consumed poison out of fear’: Officer's suicide rocks Punjab; minister resigns
content: Punjab Transport Minister Laljit Singh Bhullar resigned after a district manager died by suicide, leaving a video purportedly blaming the minister. Opposition parties allege harassment and demand a CBI probe, while Bhullar denies the accusations and calls for a fair investigation.

title: 'Biwi ki suno sirf toh ...': Newlywed Kuldeep Yadav makes honest admission
content: At the Times of India Sports Awards 2025, Kuldeep Yadav and Harmanpreet Kaur won Cricketer of the Year. Spinner Kuldeep impressed with match-winning spin performances, while batter Harmanpreet led with aggressive captaincy and consistency. The awards reflected Indian cricket’s strong 2025, with both teams achieving success and rising global dominance.

title: Big IPL warning for Vaibhav Sooryavanshi: 'All bowlers will be ready'
content: Rajasthan Royals' Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, a 14-year-old batting sensation, faces a crucial IPL 2026 season. Following a stellar Under-19 World Cup, former India all-rounder Irfan Pathan highlights the importance of this phase for the young prodigy. Pathan believes opposition teams are closely analyzing Sooryavanshi's game, making this season a significant learning curve for him to evolve and prove his mettle.

title: 'God will punish him': Karnataka social welfare department official dies by suicide, blames senior
content: A 60-year-old assistant director, set to retire soon, was found dead in his office in Pavagada. In a WhatsApp video, he blamed his joint director for harassment, stating it pushed him to commit suicide. Police have registered an abetment case against the joint director based on a complaint by the deceased's wife.

title: Your guide to men's March Madness Day 3: Recaps, every winner's chance to advance
content: Here's how every second-round game was won Saturday, plus how each winner can advance beyond the Sweet 16.

title: Huskers survive after Vanderbilt's heave rims out
content: Nebraska outlasted Vanderbilt 74-72 in a scintillating second-round NCAA tournament game Saturday night when Tyler Tanner's heave from the half-court line rimmed out at the buzzer.

title: Sources: Lakers appeal tech with Luka facing ban
content: Luka Doncic faces a one-game suspension after a verbal exchange with Orlando's Goga Bitadze earned him his 16th technical foul of the season Saturday night. Sources told ESPN the Lakers have already filed an appeal.

title: 'Four more to go': KD passes MJ on scoring list
content: Kevin Durant passed Michael Jordan for fifth on the NBA's career scoring list after finishing with 27 points in a win over the Heat.

title: Miller: Texas not Cinderella despite Sweet 16 run
content: Texas went from the NCAA tournament's First Four to the Sweet 16, but coach Sean Miller said his team isn't a Cinderella after upsetting No. 3 seed Gonzaga on Saturday.

title: Sources: UNC, Davis discussing future after loss
content: UNC officials are in discussions with coach Hubert Davis about the coach's future after another first-round loss in the NCAA tournament.

title: Brady shines, Team USA dominates flag football
content: Tom Brady started out like the GOAT of flag football before Team USA dominated the Fanatics Flag Football Classic on Saturday.

title: Leafs G Stolarz out after taking puck to throat
content: Toronto Maple Leafs goaltender Anthony Stolarz missed his expected start in Saturday's loss after taking a puck to the throat during warmups.

title: USC saved by review, then topples Clemson in OT
content: Saved by a review that nullified Clemson's buzzer-beater at the end of regulation, USC went on to win 71-67 in overtime to advance to the second round of the women's NCAA tournament.

content: Israel's military said it was not able to intercept missiles that hit the southern cities of Dimona and Arad, the largest neara nuclear facility.

content: While the full scale of damage to US assets in the Gulf region is unclear, the $800 million figure offers insight into what a prolonged conflict may cost the US.

content: The proposal aims to address Mumbai's 498 identified flooding spots through a combination of structural upgrades and innovative nature-based solutions.

content: Per the Indian embassy in Tel Aviv, the main waves of immigration into Israel from India took place in the 1950s and 1960s.

title: Hyderabad Doctor Dies By Suicide After Family Objects To Adopting A Cat
content: The incident took place on Friday evening when the doctor, identified as T Princy alias Shreshtha, was alone at home, officials said.

content: Iran launched two 4,000-kilometer-range ballistic missiles at the US-UK military base Diego Garcia in the Indian Ocean, Israeli military chief Eyal Zamir said.

content: Blowing up nuclear sites could spread radioactive material across a large area and threaten human life and health.

content: Non-compliance with the new standards could lead to de-rostering, grounding, and loss of pay for the affected personnel.

content: Dimona and Arad, two Israeli towns were hit by Iran on Saturday night in what is being called a retaliatory move for strikes on its nuclear site at Natanz.

title: Ticket Prices Likely To Soar As Centre Lifts Domestic Airfare Cap
content: The cap had been imposed after ticket prices rose following the IndiGo disruptions in December last year.

content: Aviation Turbine Fuel is the refined petroleum product used to power aircraft and typically accounts for 35 to 45% of an airline's operating costs.

content: Astrologer Ashok Kharat is accused of raping a woman and exploiting several others. State women's commission chief Rupali Chakankar resigned after outrage over her alleged links with him.

content: State Congress president S. Supongmeren Jamir formally handed over the symbol to Mr. Ao at the party's State headquarters in the presence of working presidents Khriedi Theunuo and C. Apok Jamir

content: The Puthalapattu circle police said the car, bearing a Karnataka registration number, hit a 10-wheeler lorry as the driver reportedly felt drowsy

content: In Kerala, Rahul Gandhi will kick off his election campaign for the upcoming Assembly polls in Kerala on March 25 with a major rally in Kozhikode.

title: Tamil Nadu Assembly election 2026: a game of four
content: The Tamil Nadu Assembly election will see a four-cornered contest this April. As the DMK and its allies and the AIADMK and its partners remain the principal participants of the big fight, it is also clear that the newly-formed Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam and the steady presence of the Naam Tamizhar Katchi in electoral politics will have roles to play. This report analyses the political situation ahead of this election that entices voters with the promise that it will be like nothing the State has never seen so far.

title: Jharkhand Police seek army help to defuse suspected WWII-era bomb
content: East Singhbhum SP (Rural) Rishav Garg said the bomb detection and disposal squad (BDDS) from Ranchi inspected the site in Baharagora on Thursday and found the device to be "active" and heavy.

title: Rice, wheat procurement remains ‘consistently’ low; House panel raises concern
content: Kanimozhi-led panel asks Union Food Ministry to strengthen its procurement planning and coordination with States to minimise the gap between estimated and actual procurement

title: DU’s job fair guidelines stir row over ‘routine exclusion’ of Open Learning students
content: The protesters argue that students of the School of Open Learning are usually from marginalised communities and deserve the same opportunities. There are currently around 4 lakh SOL students, according to officials.

title: Tamil Nadu Assembly election: CPI(M) State Committee stands firm on six seats
content: The CPI(M)’s contention is that it had been promised an increase in the seats for the 2021 Tamil Nadu Assembly election by the DMK leadership. On the other hand, the DMK argues that it has included more political parties in the alliance and is likely to lose 15 constituencies from its share

content: The epicenter of the earthquake was in Mangan district at 14 km below the surface at 8.41 p.m. on Saturday (March 21, 2026)

content: Home Minister G. Parameshwara has assured the House that the government will comply with the direction

content: The Prime Minister said today is also a day to appreciate those who engage in sustainable practices, promote awareness and nurture a culture of conservation

content: The project will help rid the town of its perennial drinking water scarcity, the Chairman of the 20-Point Programme said

title: T.N. Assembly election: For second time, Thoothukudi’s Pottalurani residents to boycott polls over fish waste processing units
content: The residents have been demanding the closure of fish waste processing units near the village since 2019, and have submitted multiple petitions in this regard. However, the protest intensified in early 2024 ahead of the Lok Sabha election

title: T.N. Assembly election: parties must avoid excessive freebies, focus on development, says AIADMK leader Semmalai
content: Contending that these handouts being announced as part of electoral promises by major parties will not facilitate T.N.’s development, he criticises the DMK government for not increasing the capital expenditure substantially over the last 5 years and ‘frittering away’ funds for distributing freebies

title: Meghalaya leader of Opposition Mukul Sangma demands independent inquiry into Garo Hills violence
content: In a petition to Governor C.H. Vijayashankar, Mr. Sangma flagged a "serious law and order situation" in the Garo Hills region, which he said stemmed from a series of developments linked to the elections to the tribal Garo Hills Autonomous District Council

title: SIR fallout obliterated localised anti-incumbency in West Bengal, no discontent against Mamata: Sagarika Ghose
content: In its list of 291 candidates, declared by Ms. Banerjee on March 17, the TMC dropped 74 sitting MLAs, nearly a third of its legislative strength, signaling a calibrated counter anti-incumbency strategy

content: The new norms were issued on March 22, 2026 by the Municipal Administration and Urban Development (MA&UD) Department.

content: Why did the Supreme Court strike down Section 60(4) of the Social Security Code, 2020? What did it say about maternity leave for adoptive mothers? What did the Court recommend that the Union government do regarding paternity leave?

content: Naam Tamilar Katchi chief coordinator Seeman contends that without discipline, there will be no order; he bats for an economy centred on agriculture in T.N. and plans to create government-led industries based on local resources if his party emerges victorious in the Assembly election

title: No blanket exemption for women teachers above 50 from SIR and census: Revenue Department
content: A senior woman teacher at a government school in Bengaluru says that teachers will meet School Education and Literacy Minister Madhu Bangarappa and seek a blanket exemption again.

title: Without nature, human life is unimaginable, says Hemant Soren on Sharhul
content: Jharkhand Chief Minister asserted that had nature not existed, there would be no living beings neither humans nor animals in the world

title: Centre removes caps on airfares after three months
content: The removal of the caps, introduced when widespread cancellation of IndiGo flights triggered a spike in fares, comes at a time when West Asia conflict has led to increase in aviation turbine fuel (ATF)

title: RDG discontinuation fallout: Himachal presents ‘reduced’ budget
content: The Chief Minister started his budget presentation by accusing the BJP for not supporting the State’s interests; Milk procurement price hiked by ₹10

title: BJD suspends six MLAs for defying party’s whip in Rajya Sabha election
content: Regional party’s tally in Odisha Legislative Assembly has come down to 42 from 51 seats won in 2024 assembly election

title: Punjab Minister Bhullar resigns from Cabinet as Opposition demands murder case, CBI probe
content: Laljit Singh Bhullar has been accused of abetting suicide of Gagandeep Singh Randhawa, district manager of the Punjab Warehousing Corporation in Amritsar

title: KLU releases Management Aptitude Test results
content: The university will conduct an entrance examination for engineering courses for three days from March 27

title: Untreated waste, poor civic infra mar Erode (East) Assembly constituency
content: Pollution, traffic congestion, and poor urban infrastructure remain key concerns for voters, who are looking for concrete outcomes

title: Religious fervour and prayers for harmony mark Eid-Ul-Fitr
content: Andhra Pradesh Governor S. Abdul Nazeer conveys Eid greetings; Eidgah Committee calls for promotion of humanity over caste and religious differences

title: Supreme Court concerned over cops uploading videos online, says poses threat to fair trial
content: The senior counsel flagged the recent trend of police posting the images of accused persons being handcuffed, tied by ropes, paraded, forced to kneel, etc, on social media, which, apart from causing an affront to personal dignity, adds to the public bias.

title: Process of issuance of PRs to FMGs begins in Andhra Pradesh
content: Around 30 graduates who approached the High Court on the delay have received PRs from March 12 till date; uncertainty continues as the APMC asks for Indian Embassy’s apostille on compensatory certificates

title: IIT Madras launches M.Tech and MA programmes in frontier technologies and governance
content: The programmes have been designed to equip students with interdisciplinary skills and knowledge to address challenges in the fields of governance, industry, and technology

title: SRMIST honours scientists in advanced materials research
content: This was part of the second International Conference on Advanced Functional Materials and Devices organised by its Nanotechnology Research Centre

title: Assembly election highlights: BJP out to claim its share in Bihar: Prashant Kishor on Nitish's exit as CM
content: The Election Commission of India announces the completion of the first randomisation of EVMs and VVPATs in Assam, Kerala, and Puducherry

title: Vellore Mayor, councillor booked for violating Model Code of Conduct
content: Inquiry reveals that along with the local ward councillor, Mayor Sujatha Anandakumar allegedly distributed government-sponsored sports equipment to youth in a corporation office at Sathuvachari

title: ‘India’s gem, jewellery sector shows resilience’
content: Mr. Bhansali said reaching ₹2.28 lakh crore in exports with over 4% growth between April 2025 and February 2026, despite a 44% dip in traditional markets like the U.S. was a testament to the industry’s successful strategy of market diversification

title: PM Modi speaks to Iran President; calls for ‘freedom of navigation’ in the Gulf
content: India has so far evacuated around 882 Indian businessmen, students and pilgrims from Iran through neighbouring Armenia and Azerbaijan, MEA Spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal said on March 20

title: ‘No option but to strike Mumbai and Delhi’: Ex-Pak envoy to India on ‘worst-case’ US conflict

title: 'The best is still yet to come': Why Wyatt Langford is an AL MVP sleeper pick
content: outfielder Wyatt Langford landed at no. 4 on the top 100 prospects list of ESPN's Kiley McDaniel . he had spent just 44 games in the minor leagues at that point, yet broke camp with the defending champions as their every-day left fielder . his rapid development meant heightened external expectations .

title: Women's March Madness Day 2 recap: Saturday's first-round action
content: UTSA Roadrunners vs. UConn Huskies: Game Highlights (1:19) The first round of the 2026 women's NCAA tournament is in the books. Thirty-two teams have advanced to the second round, which opens Sunday (noon ET, ESPN). We tracked all 16 games from Storrs, Connecticut, to Los Angeles, as our reporters broke down the action.

title: Power Rankings: New 1-32 poll, points pace vs. preseason projections
content: a panel of ESPN hockey commentators, analysts, reporters and editors send in a 1-32 poll based on the games through Wednesday . we have listed each team's preseason over/under total (per DraftKings Sportsbook) as well as its current points pace .

title: Ranking the remaining 32 teams in women's March Madness
content: only three lower-seeded teams won games in the first two days of the tournament . the Cavaliers are just the second team since the field expanded to 68 teams in 2022 to go from the first four to the second round . south Carolina 's 69-point trouncing was the most statistically impressive victory among the top seeds .

title: Women's March Madness Day 2 takeaways: How UConn, USC, Virginia won
content: how the Bruins won: After a competitive first half, UCLA came out of halftime and scored the first 11 points of the third quarter . their plus-27 point differential is their second best since quarters were adopted in 2015-16 .
